_comment: CERNIQ BATE / webhook worker — separate Railway service from the API. Shares the same Postgres + Redis. No public ingress; do NOT attach a domain.
_planTier: Hobby is sufficient until BATE recompute lag (Grafana panel: bate_recompute_lag_seconds) routinely exceeds 60s, then upsize to Pro for committed CPU.
_region: us-east — must match the API service so Redis and Postgres traffic stays intra-region.

build._nixpacksAlternative: If using NIXPACKS instead, set buildCommand to mirror the API service and override startCommand to `node apps/api/dist/workers/main.js`.

deploy._healthcheckNote: No HTTP healthcheck — this is a queue consumer. Liveness is enforced by Railway's restart policy + BullMQ heartbeat. If we expose a /metrics endpoint later, add `healthcheckPath: /metrics`.
deploy._restartRationale: Higher retry count than the API because transient Redis hiccups should not page the operator; the worker can re-enter the queue safely thanks to BullMQ's at-least-once delivery + the audit chain's idempotent append.
deploy._scaleNote: Run a single worker replica until BATE signal volume justifies sharding by queue. When that happens, split into per-queue services (bate-worker, webhook-worker, signal-worker) rather than scaling this one horizontally — BullMQ's `concurrency` already gives intra-process parallelism.

envVars._note: Override per replica when sharding queues: bate | webhooks | signals | all.

envVars._provision: Same connection string as the API service — share via Railway's reference variable: ${{ cerniq-api.DATABASE_URL }}.
Mirror of the API's DATABASE_DIRECT_URL. The worker may run lightweight read queries that are friendlier to a direct connection than to pgbouncer transaction-pool mode.
Reference: ${{ cerniq-api.REDIS_URL }}. Sharing the cache instance is intentional — BullMQ requires it.
Same key as the API. The worker writes audit events for asynchronous outcomes (webhook delivery results, BATE score changes); the chain stays unbroken because both services sign with the same key.
Use a SEPARATE Sentry project from the API so worker errors don't drown the API issue list.
Same collector endpoint as the API; the collector multiplexes by service.name.
